episodes.title: Torre doubts source of Quiboloy wealth: 'Saan nanggagaling ‘yung pera?' | DZMM TeleRadyo
episodes.description:
Courtesy of DZMM Teleradyo.

Retired Philippine National Police chief Nicolas Torre III raised questions about the source of televangelist Apollo Quiboloy’s wealth, saying the number of members in his rallies or in the Kingdom of Jesus Christ Compound do not even reach a million.

Torre said only a thousand people showed up at the KOJC Compound when he arrested Quiboloy in September 2024.

“Ang sinasabi ko lang po nung tayo ay nasa Davao para i-implement ‘yung warrant of arrest kay Quiboloy, hindi ho nakita ‘yung sinasabi niyang napakaraming tagasunod dahil kung sinasabi niyang milyon ang kanyang tagasunod sa buong mundo ni Quiboloy sana ay sa 16 days na hinanahanap natin siya sa KOJC Compound e di sana yung nagra-rally dun wala nang tigil kung milyon-milyon nga ang kanyang tauhan pero wala tayong nakikitang ganun,” he said.

“Hindi talaga ma-explain kung sa side ko bakit ganun karami lang ‘yung taong nakita ko dun, mga 1,000 lang yun.”

He added only 2,400 people showed up during the KOJC’s 39th anniversary last September 1, 2025 compared to the group’s claim that they have millions of members. 

“Totoo nga may mga nagsidatingan worldwide na may mga panaka-nakang dumating galing sa ibang bansa na mga pastor nila, mga ministro nila at mga miyembro nila galing sa ibang bansa pero ang total count nila noong gabing iyon 2,400 tapos ang iba dun na-detect namin ay mga hakot lang na taong galing kalapit probinsya,” he said.

Torre said the number of KOJC members in their meeting raises serious questions about how Quiboloy could build a large compound in Davao City as well as purchase a private plane that was used by former president Rodrigo Duterte.

“Kung hindi galing sa mga miyembro ang ginamit niya para sa kanyang mga imprastraktura…saan nanggagaling yung pera?”

“Ang sinasabi ko lang - baka walang kakayahan si Quiboloy na bilhin lahat ng mga ‘yan kung ang basehan lang natin ay ‘yung dami ng kanyang mga supporters.”

episodes.title: Pasig court orders inspection of Apollo Quiboloy's KOJC properties - The World Tonight
Unification Church leader on trial over graft charges | ABS-CBN News
episodes.description:
SEOUL, South Korea - The leader of the Unification Church goes on trial on Monday, accused of bribing South Korea's former first lady with gifts including a designer handbag and a diamond necklace.
Han Hak-ja's arrest in September rocked the cult-like church, which claims to have 10 million followers worldwide and controls a sprawling business empire.
The 82-year-old defendant, known to her followers as "holy mother", also faces graft charges over cash payments to a lawmaker linked to disgraced president Yoon Suk Yeol.
Han has disputed the charges, telling her followers in a video message in August that "false claims are being spread that our church provided illegal political funds on my orders.
"I want to state this clearly here... I have never ordered any unlawful political request or monetary transaction."
- 'Only begotten daughter' -
Han took over leadership of the Unification Church after the death of her husband Moon Sun-myung, who founded the assembly in 1954 after he was rejected by mainstream Protestant churches.
Moon claimed to be the Second Coming of Jesus Christ, while the church has a rigid, hierarchical culture and its followers are sometimes derisively referred to as "Moonies".
Han, who has 14 children with Moon, is referred to by followers as God's "only begotten daughter" and the "holy mother".
The church rose to global prominence in the 1970s and 80s, becoming known for mass weddings often held in stadiums.
Over the decades, it amassed a business empire that spans construction, food, education and the media, including the ownership of Washington Times and Sunmoon University.
However, it also came under repeated scrutiny over how it obtained financial donations from members and its links to politicians.
Japan this year took legal action to order the Japanese chapter of the Unification Church dissolved, after the gunman who killed former prime minister Shinzo Abe was believed to have targeted him over grudges against the church.
In Seoul, Han will have to answer allegations that she gave luxury goods worth around 82 million won ($56,000) to Yoon's wife Kim Keon Hee, herself under arrest over charges of bribery and stock-market manipulation.
Han is also suspected of having conspired to pay 100 million won to a People's Party MP in 2022 in a bid to seek favour with Yoon, who went on to win the presidency that year.
A separate trial will open on December 9 with Han facing charges that she violated the political parties act.
Prosecutors believe she had directed more than 2,000 church members to sign up for Yoon's People Power Party ahead of a party convention to influence its outcome.
Han was briefly released in November to undergo eye surgery but has returned to pre-trial custody. She is seeking bail.
"We explained to the court that the charges against Han differ from the facts, and fully detailed the health difficulties she faces due to her age and medical conditions," the Unification Church told AFP in a statement.
"Proper treatment and recovery are essential for uncovering the truth," it added.
kjk/hmn/mtp

episodes.title: EP 11: A Fourteen-Year Manhunt: The Ecleo Murder Case | Philippines’ Most Shocking Stories
episodes.description:
Philippines' Most Shocking Stories | Season 2 Episode 1: The Ruben Ecleo Murder Case

Hosted by reporter Lyza Aquino, “A Fourteen-Year Manhunt” revisits one of the most infamous criminal scandals in recent Philippine history: the 2002 murder of Alona Bacolod-Ecleo, fourth-year medical student and wife of cult leader Ruben Ecleo Jr. Her body was found wrapped in a black garbage bag and dumped in a ravine in Dalaguete, Cebu. The case exploded into public view when her family members were also brutally murdered, allegedly to silence key witnesses.

“Philippines’ Most Shocking Stories” is a YouTube series from ABS-CBN News and Current Affairs that looks back into real-life events that stunned the nation — stories that made headlines, triggered investigations and sparked public outcry. The series is hosted by ABS-CBN News reporters who provide new details, fresh perspectives and a comprehensive picture on what happened in these heart-breaking tragedies, true crime cases and national scandals.

Don't forget to like, share and leave your own theories in the comments below.

New episodes will be uploaded every Tuesday at 8 PM.

episodes.title: Risa Hontiveros sues Michael Maurilio for cyberlibel | ABS-CBN News
episodes.description:
Sen. Risa Hontiveros on Wednesday (July 9) filed a cyber libel complaint at the Department of Justice (DOJ) against Michael Maurilio alias "Rene," the former Senate witness against Kingdom of Jesus Christ (KOJC) leader Apollo Quiboloy. Maurilio had claimed that he was bribed by Hontiveros to testify against Quiboloy, former President Rodrigo Duterte and Vice President Sara Duterte.

episodes.title: 'Maniningil kami,' Risa Hontiveros warns those endangering witnesses in Quiboloy probe
episodes.description:
Sen. Risa Hontiveros on Monday (June 30) said her office will file a criminal complaint against those behind the video exposing the witnesses in her committee's probe into alleged crimes involving Pastor Apollo Quiboloy, as well as a former witness, Michael Maurilio, who now claims to have been bribed by the lawmaker for his testimony. In her statement, Hontiveros detailed messages between her office and Maurilio as evidence of his voluntary participation in the investigation.

episodes.title: Witness in Quiboloy probe alleges bribery; Hontiveros readies 'serious legal response' | ANC
episodes.description:
Sen. Risa Hontiveros said Thursday (June 26) she was preparing a “serious legal response” against an alleged witness who claimed he was paid by the lawmaker to give false testimonies against Pastor Apollo Quiboloy, former President Rodrigo Duterte, and Vice President Sara Duterte during the Senate hearings last year.
“We are preparing our serious legal response against this act of harassment and intimidation. Hindi namin palalampasin ang mga nasa likod ng pananakot na ito,” Hontiveros said in a statement.
In a video posted on the Facebook page Pagtanggol Valiente, a certain Michael Maurilio who uses the alias “Rene”, said he was speaking voluntarily to set the record straight.

episodes.title: Hontiveros: Over 1,000 minors under 'cult' control in Surigao del Norte | ABS-CBN News
episodes.description:
A Philippine senator revealed Monday, September 18 that a "cult" in Socorro, Surigao del Norte has been victimizing over 1,000 minors and subjecting them to "rape, sexual violence, child abuse, and forced marriage."

Sen. Risa Hontiveros said based on her team's research and other testimonies, it all started when a cult leader supposedly "groomed" a 17-year-old to become the next "messiah."

She also revealed that the cult is not just limited to the province of Surigao, but is also expanding to Manila and other parts of the country.

—Report by Arra Perez, ABS-CBN News
